body.label: source.root.theorem
body.claim:
Let $X = \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}$, and let $\mathcal{B} = \{ B_n \mid n \in X \}$, where $B_n = \{ k \cdot n \mid k \in \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1} \}$. In the topology on $X$ generated by this basis, for every $m \in X$, the closure of the singleton $\{m\}$ is
\[
\overline{\{m\}} = \{ d \in X \mid d \text{ divides } m \}.
\]

proof_steps.text: No proof is given in this fragment that \(x\in D(m)\).

proof_steps.proof_method: This is the remaining goal for the fixed arbitrary element x under the current hypotheses.

proof_steps.text: For this arbitrary \(x\), one must show \(x\in D(m)\).

proof_steps.proof_method: This is stated as the remaining goal to be shown.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: For a set defined by comprehension, a point belongs to the set exactly when it satisfies the defining conditions.
deduced_from_theorem.name: Set-builder notation membership criterion
deduced_from_theorem.description: Used to unfold membership in D(m).

proof_steps.claim: x\in D(m) amounts to proving that x\in X and x divides m.
proof_steps.proof_method: Unfold the definition D(m)=\{d\in X\mid d\text{ divides }m\}.

proof_steps.text: Since \(D(m)=\{d\in X\mid d\text{ divides }m\}\), proving \(x\in D(m)\) amounts to proving that \(x\in X\) and \(x\) divides \(m\).

proof_steps.proof_method: This is the remaining membership goal for the arbitrary x.

proof_steps.text: The proof must show that this arbitrary \(x\) satisfies \(x\in D(m)\).

proof_steps.proof_method: This is stated as the remaining goal to be shown.
by the definition of D(m), after showing that x divides m

proof_steps.text: It remains to show that this arbitrary element belongs to \(D(m)\).

proof_steps.proof_method: This is the remaining goal to prove from the current hypotheses.
This is stated as the remaining goal to prove.

proof_steps.label: source.root.theorem.proof.root.incl_closure_subset_D.arbitrary_element.arbitrary_element_setup.arbitrary_x.membership_obligation

proof_steps.proof_method: This is the remaining proof obligation for the fixed arbitrary element x.

proof_steps.text: The subsequent proof must show that this arbitrary \(x\) lies in \(D(m)\).

proof_steps.label: source.root.theorem.proof.root.incl_closure_subset_D.arbitrary_element.membership_in_Dm

proof_steps.proof_method: To prove by the arbitrary choice of x and the hypotheses; the source fragment does not provide the argument that x divides m.

proof_steps.text: From the arbitrary choice of x, prove x\in D(m).

proof_steps.label: source.root.theorem.proof.root.incl_closure_subset_D.closure_neighborhood
proof_steps.claim: \forall n\in X,\; x\in B_n \implies m\in B_n

deduced_from_claim: B_n is a basic neighborhood of x

deduced_from_theorem.claim: A point x lies in the closure of a set A if and only if every neighborhood of x intersects A.
deduced_from_theorem.name: Closure neighborhood characterization
deduced_from_theorem.description: Applied to A={m}; since {m} is a singleton, intersection of every neighborhood of x with {m} implies m lies in every basic neighborhood of x.

proof_steps.claim: x\in D(m) \iff x\in X \land x \mid m
proof_steps.proof_method: By the closure condition, every basic neighborhood B_n of x contains m.

proof_steps.text: By the closure condition, every basic neighborhood B_n of x contains m.

proof_steps.label: source.root.theorem.proof.root.incl_closure_subset_D.use_B_x

deduced_from_claim: m\in B_n for every basic neighborhood B_n of x
proof_steps.proof_method: Take n=x; since x\in B_x, B_x is a basic neighborhood of x, so the universal neighborhood condition gives m\in B_x.

proof_steps.text: Taking n=x, since x\in B_x, we get m\in B_x.

proof_steps.label: source.root.theorem.proof.root.incl_closure_subset_D.divisibility

proof_steps.proof_method: Unfold the definition of B_x and use m\in B_x.
By the definition of divisibility, since m is an integer multiple of x.
Unfold the definition of D(m) and use that x divides m.

proof_steps.text: Since m\in B_x, we have m=kx for some k\ge 1. Hence x divides m, so x\in D(m).

proof_steps.label: source.root.theorem.proof.root.incl_D_subset_closure.neighborhood_intersection
proof_steps.claim: \forall U,\; U \text{ is an open neighborhood of } x \implies U\cap\{m\}\ne\varnothing
\forall U,\; U \text{ is an open neighborhood of } x \implies U\cap\{m\}\ne\varnothing

proof_steps.label: source.root.theorem.proof.root.incl_D_subset_closure.closure_membership

deduced_from_claim: \text{Every open neighborhood } U \text{ of } x \text{ satisfies } U\cap\{m\}\ne\varnothing

deduced_from_theorem.claim: For a subset A of a topological space, x\in\overline{A} if and only if every open neighborhood of x intersects A.
deduced_from_theorem.name: Neighborhood characterization of closure

proof_steps.proof_method: By the neighborhood characterization of closure, since every open neighborhood of x meets \{m\}.

proof_steps.text: Since every open neighborhood of x meets {m}, x belongs to the closure of {m}.
